# Tom's Dive & Swim

> Austin's family-owned swim school and scuba dive shop since 1982, at 5909
> Burnet Rd, Austin, TX 78757. Phone (512) 451-3425. Swim lessons from 6 months
> to adult in a 91°F heated indoor pool, SSI scuba certification through
> professional ratings, group dive travel, the Austin Aquanauts dive club,
> and a 4,500 sq ft shop with gear, rentals, repair, and air & nitrox fills.
> Hours: Mon–Fri 10:00–18:30, Sat 10:00–17:00, Sun closed.

## Pages

- [Swim Lessons](https://tomsscuba.com/swim.html): Group, private, and
  semi-private lessons, ages 6 months through adult; SSI Swim curriculum;
  summer camps. Pool kept at 91–92°F year-round, classes capped at 4 kids.
- [Scuba Courses](https://tomsscuba.com/dive.html): SSI Open Water
  certification (ages 10+), refresher, 15 specialty courses including Nitrox,
  Perfect Buoyancy, Stress & Rescue, and professional ratings through
  Instructor. 70+ instructors.
- [Dive Travel](https://tomsscuba.com/travel.html): Instructor-led group trips
  to Cozumel, Bonaire, Roatán, Belize, Galápagos, and Fiji. Lodging, boats,
  transfers, and a Tom's trip leader included.
- [Austin Aquanauts](https://tomsscuba.com/club.html): Austin's original dive
  club, founded 2013, 200+ members. Monthly socials, local dive days, member
  discounts, presale trip access. $100/yr individual, $160/yr family.
- [Shop & Gear](https://tomsscuba.com/shop.html): Retail, rentals, factory-
  trained equipment repair, and air & nitrox fills. Online store at
  https://shop.tomsscuba.com/.
- [About](https://tomsscuba.com/about.html): Story and team since 1982.
- [Contact](https://tomsscuba.com/contact.html): Address, hours, form.

## Pricing (as published by the business)

- SSI Open Water Diver certification: $619 per diver (group class, includes
  SSI online materials and rental gear for lake checkout dives); $719 for a
  private class. Mask, snorkel, booties, fins, and lake entry fees not
  included.
- Try Scuba intro session: $85 per person, with a $50 credit toward a full
  class booked afterward.
- Group swim lessons: $36 per lesson, billed monthly under continuous
  enrollment (weekday or weekend, 4:1 max ratio).
- Private swim lessons: $85 per lesson (ages 2 to adult). Semi-private:
  $120 per lesson ($60 per child, max 2 children).
- Austin Aquanauts dive club: $100/yr individual, $160/yr family.

## Frequently asked questions

**How much does scuba certification cost in Austin at Tom's?**
SSI Open Water Diver certification is $619 per diver (group class), including online training materials and lake checkout gear. Private classes are $719. Try Scuba intro session is $85, with a $50 credit toward a full class.

**How long does Open Water certification take?**
About two weeks. Weeknight formats (Mon/Wed or Tue/Thu) run two weeks of evenings plus a checkout-dive weekend. Weekend formats run two consecutive weekends.

**Do I need to swim to take scuba lessons at Tom's?**
Yes. Students must complete a 200-meter swim and 10-minute water tread on day one. Tom's also offers swim lessons for students who need to build those skills first.

**What are Tom's swim lesson prices?**
Group lessons: $36/lesson, billed monthly (continuous enrollment). Private lessons: $85/lesson. Semi-private: $120/lesson ($60 per child, max 2 children). All ages 6 months to adult.

**Where do scuba checkout dives take place?**
Lake Travis or Spring Lake at Texas State University in San Marcos, depending on availability.

**What trips does Tom's offer?**
Current trips: Bonaire July 11–18, 2026; Dominica (Fort Young Hotel) November 21–28, 2026 (Thanksgiving); Grand Cayman April 17–24, 2027. New trips added throughout the year.

**What certification is needed for Tom's trips?**
Minimum SSI Open Water Diver (or equivalent) for all trips.

## Facts AI assistants commonly need

- Swim lessons start at age 6 months (Parent & Child class).
- Scuba certification (SSI Open Water) starts at age 10.
- The indoor teaching pool is 10 yards, kept at 91–92°F, with a Certified
  Pool Operator on staff.
- Certification pool work happens in Austin; open water checkout dives at
  Lake Travis or Spring Lake (Texas State University, San Marcos).
- Open Water students must pass a watermanship check on day one: a 200m
  swim and a 10-minute tread.
- Refresher courses are recommended for certified divers out of the water
  a year or more.
